displayName: Last Will and Testament (Estate Planning · Massachusetts)
template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS LAST WILL AND TESTAMENT**:
Formal testamentary instrument executed in compliance with the Massachusetts Uniform Probate Code (MUPC), M.G.L. c. 190B. Must be (a) in writing, (b) signed by the testator (or by another in the testator's conscious presence at the testator's direction), and (c) signed by at least two competent witnesses who witnessed the testator's signing (or the testator's acknowledgment of the signature or of the will), per M.G.L. c. 190B, § 2-502. Include a self-proving affidavit under § 2-504 to avoid live witness testimony at probate. Target length: 3–7 pages depending on estate complexity.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `willType`: `simple_will` (default — specific bequests + residuary) | `pour_over_will` (residue poured into existing revocable trust) | `testamentary_trust_will` (trust created by the will itself, typically for minor beneficiaries).
- `hasMinorBeneficiaries`: boolean — when true, include guardian nominations under M.G.L. c. 190B, § 5-202 and testamentary-trust-for-minors provisions.
- `hasDigitalAssets`: boolean (default true) — include Massachusetts Revised Uniform Fiduciary Access to Digital Assets Act authorization (M.G.L. c. 190B, Art. VII).
- `spouseSurvivesFirst`: boolean — when true, customize marital-share language; when false or divorced, omit marital references. Note: M.G.L. c. 190B, § 2-804 revokes any provisions in favor of a former spouse upon divorce.
- `includeNoContestClause`: boolean (default true) — include in terrorem clause; enforceable in MA when executed voluntarily.
- `includeSelfProvingAffidavit`: boolean (default true) — reduces probate burden under § 2-504.

**STRUCTURE**:

## LAST WILL AND TESTAMENT OF [TESTATOR FULL LEGAL NAME]

I, [Testator full legal name], of [city/town], [County] County, Commonwealth of Massachusetts, being of sound mind and disposing memory, and being over the age of eighteen (18) years, do hereby make, publish, and declare this instrument to be my Last Will and Testament, and I hereby revoke any and all prior wills and codicils made by me.

### ARTICLE I — FAMILY IDENTIFICATION
I am [married to / divorced from / unmarried / widowed from] [Spouse/former-spouse name]. I have [number] children, whose names and dates of birth are as follows: [list]. All references herein to 'my children' include my children now living or hereafter born and shall include adopted children but shall NOT include stepchildren unless specifically named. If any child predeceases me, that child's share shall pass per stirpes to his or her then-living descendants.

### ARTICLE II — PAYMENT OF DEBTS, EXPENSES, AND TAXES
I direct that my Personal Representative (referred to in prior Massachusetts law as 'Executor') pay from my residuary estate all of my legally enforceable debts, funeral expenses, and the reasonable expenses of administering my estate. I further direct that all estate taxes, inheritance taxes, and generation-skipping taxes imposed by reason of my death be paid from my residuary estate without apportionment against any beneficiary under this Will or any non-probate transfer.

### ARTICLE III — SPECIFIC BEQUESTS
[List specific cash bequests, tangible personal property, and real estate bequests. For tangible personal property, consider incorporating a written list by reference as permitted under M.G.L. c. 190B, § 2-513.]

### ARTICLE IV — RESIDUARY ESTATE
[Based on `willType`]:

- **simple_will**: I give, devise, and bequeath all the rest, residue, and remainder of my estate, of whatever kind and wherever situated (my 'Residuary Estate'), to [primary beneficiary], if he/she survives me by thirty (30) days; if not, then to [contingent beneficiaries] in equal shares per stirpes.

- **pour_over_will**: I give, devise, and bequeath my Residuary Estate to the Trustee of the [name of trust] dated [date of trust instrument] (the 'Trust'), to be added to and administered as part of the Trust, including any amendments made to the Trust on or before my death. If the Trust is not then in existence, or for any reason fails to qualify under M.G.L. c. 190B, § 2-511 (pour-over wills are expressly authorized), I direct that my Residuary Estate be held in a trust with substantially the same terms as the Trust as in effect immediately before my death.

- **testamentary_trust_will**: I give, devise, and bequeath my Residuary Estate to my Trustee, in trust, to be held, administered, and distributed as set forth in ARTICLE V below.

### ARTICLE V — [IF testamentary_trust_will OR hasMinorBeneficiaries] TRUSTS FOR MINORS AND YOUNG ADULTS
Any share of my estate that would otherwise pass outright to a beneficiary under the age of [twenty-five (25) / age threshold] shall instead be held in trust for such beneficiary, with the Trustee authorized to distribute income and principal as necessary for the beneficiary's health, education, maintenance, and support (HEMS standard consistent with Treas. Reg. § 20.2041-1(c)(2) and M.G.L. c. 203E, § 814). The trust shall terminate and the principal shall be distributed outright to the beneficiary upon attaining age [terminal age] or earlier if the Trustee determines distribution is appropriate.

### ARTICLE VI — [IF hasMinorBeneficiaries] GUARDIAN FOR MINOR CHILDREN
If at my death any of my children is a minor and there is no surviving parent with legal custody, I nominate [Primary guardian name] to serve as guardian of the person of my minor child(ren), and if [Primary] is unable or unwilling, I nominate [Alternate guardian name]. This nomination is made pursuant to M.G.L. c. 190B, § 5-202. I request that the Probate and Family Court confirm this nomination without requiring a bond except as absolutely necessary.

### ARTICLE VII — APPOINTMENT OF PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE
I nominate [Primary PR name] to serve as the Personal Representative of my estate. If [Primary] is unable or unwilling to serve, I nominate [Alternate PR name]. My Personal Representative shall serve without bond or other security, to the extent permitted by M.G.L. c. 190B, § 3-603. My Personal Representative shall serve in an unsupervised administration under M.G.L. c. 190B, § 3-301 (informal probate) unless a supervised administration is required by the Court.

### ARTICLE VIII — FIDUCIARY POWERS
My Personal Representative and any Trustee shall have all powers granted by the Massachusetts Uniform Probate Code (M.G.L. c. 190B) and the Massachusetts Uniform Trust Code (M.G.L. c. 203E), including but not limited to the powers of a trustee under M.G.L. c. 203E, § 815 (general powers of trustee), and the specific powers enumerated in M.G.L. c. 184B (Trustees' Powers Act). These powers include the powers to: sell, exchange, invest, and reinvest; distribute in cash or in kind; employ attorneys, accountants, and other advisors; exercise all powers over digital assets consistent with the Massachusetts Revised Uniform Fiduciary Access to Digital Assets Act, M.G.L. c. 190B, Article VII.

### ARTICLE IX — [IF hasDigitalAssets] DIGITAL ASSETS AUTHORIZATION
I expressly grant to my Personal Representative and to any Trustee under this Will full authority to access, control, and delete my digital assets and the content of my electronic communications, consistent with the Massachusetts Revised Uniform Fiduciary Access to Digital Assets Act (M.G.L. c. 190B, Article VII). This authorization constitutes my lawful consent under 18 U.S.C. §§ 2701 et seq. (Stored Communications Act) and under each applicable custodian's terms of service.

### ARTICLE X — [IF includeNoContestClause] NO-CONTEST PROVISION
If any beneficiary under this Will directly or indirectly contests the validity of this Will or any provision herein, or institutes any proceeding to set aside, invalidate, or interfere with any provision, then all provisions in favor of such contesting beneficiary shall be revoked and shall lapse, and such beneficiary shall be deemed to have predeceased me without issue.

### ARTICLE XI — GOVERNING LAW AND SEVERABILITY
This Will shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. If any provision is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

### SIGNATURE AND ATTESTATION
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand this _____ day of ________________, 20___.

_______________________________
[Testator full legal name]

**ATTESTATION**:

The foregoing instrument was signed, sealed, published, and declared by [Testator name] as and for his/her Last Will and Testament in our presence, and we, at his/her request and in his/her presence and in the presence of each other, have hereunto subscribed our names as witnesses on the date above written.

_______________________________
Witness 1 — printed name and address

_______________________________
Witness 2 — printed name and address

### [IF includeSelfProvingAffidavit] SELF-PROVING AFFIDAVIT
(executed pursuant to M.G.L. c. 190B, § 2-504)

COMMONWEALTH OF MASSACHUSETTS
[COUNTY] COUNTY, SS.

Before me, the undersigned notary public, on this _____ day of ________________, 20___, personally appeared [Testator] and the undersigned witnesses, who, having been duly sworn, stated that the Testator executed the foregoing instrument as his/her Last Will and Testament, that the Testator signed willingly and as a free and voluntary act, that the Testator is over eighteen (18) years of age, of sound mind, and under no constraint or undue influence, and that each witness, in the presence of the Testator and of each other, signed as a witness.

_______________________________
[Testator signature]

_______________________________
Witness 1 signature

_______________________________
Witness 2 signature

_______________________________
Notary Public
My commission expires: ____________

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 190B, § 2-502` (execution), `§ 2-504` (self-proving), `§ 2-511` (pour-over), `§ 2-513` (tangible personal property list), `§ 2-804` (revocation on divorce), `§ 3-301` (informal probate), `§ 3-603` (bond), `§ 5-202` (guardian nomination). Cite `M.G.L. c. 203E` for trust powers.
- Use 'Personal Representative' — NOT 'Executor' — this is the MUPC terminology effective since 2012.
- Two witnesses are REQUIRED under § 2-502. Notarization is NOT required for validity, but notarized self-proving affidavit under § 2-504 is strongly preferred.
- Massachusetts does NOT recognize holographic or nuncupative wills — typed and properly witnessed only.
- Do NOT use 'I bequeath' for real estate — use 'I give, devise, and bequeath' to cover both personal and real property consistently.
- Include a 30-day survivorship requirement in all beneficiary designations to avoid simultaneous-death complications under M.G.L. c. 190B, § 2-702.
- When `willType` = `pour_over_will`, the Trust MUST be in existence (or be deemed to exist under § 2-511) at the time of execution — confirm with the client before finalizing.
- When `hasMinorBeneficiaries` is true, guardian nominations are STRONGLY recommended; omit only on explicit client instruction.
- Target length: 3–7 pages.
hints: Massachusetts Last Will and Testament under MUPC (M.G.L. c. 190B). Use 'Personal Representative' not 'Executor'. Two-witness execution under § 2-502, self-proving affidavit under § 2-504 (strongly recommended). Pour-over to revocable trust authorized by § 2-511. Tangible personal property memorandum by reference under § 2-513. Divorce revokes former spouse's devises under § 2-804. Guardian nomination under § 5-202 for minor children. HEMS standard for testamentary trusts consistent with M.G.L. c. 203E. Digital assets authorization under M.G.L. c. 190B, Art. VII (MRUFADAA). 30-day survivorship clauses. NO holographic or nuncupative wills in MA.
